---
title: Running SCTransform separately on each sample
date: 2023-05-08
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

Re-running analysis from 2023-05-03, except with normalization (`SCTransform`) on each sample before merging.


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<tumour Vladoiu software directory>/create_srat.R`.

### Output

The following bash command was run to generate the Seurat object (the working directory was set as the output directory).

```bash
$ Rscript /CBL_scRNAseq/software/tumour/Vladoiu/create_srat.R \
      --in_dir /isilon/CBL_scRNAseq-archived/data/tumour/Vladoiu/ \
      --out_dir ./ \
      &> create_srat.log
```


## Results

### QC pre-processing

No change from 2023-05-03.

### Normalization and dimensionality reduction

The results from 2023-05-03 (merge+sct) were generated by:

1. Merging the samples, then
1. Running `SCTransform`.

Today's results (sct+merge) were generated by flipping the steps:

1. Running `SCTransform` separately on each sample (with `purrr::map`, line 81), then
1. Merging the samples and setting the variable features to all the genes that are present in the SCT assay's `scale.data` slot (see [https://github.com/satijalab/seurat/issues/5135](https://github.com/satijalab/seurat/issues/5135)).

::: {#fig-umap-compare}

![(a) UMAP from 2023-05-03 (merge+sct).](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230503/umap.png)

![(b) UMAP from today (sct+merge).](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230508/umap.png)

UMAPs of cells coloured by sample (left) and cluster (right). UMAP was run using the top 50 PCs.

:::

Interestingly, running sct+merge (@fig-umap-compare&#8288;b) results in the samples clustering together more closely compared to merge+sct (@fig-umap-compare&#8288;a).

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| cache: false
#| cache-lazy: false

library(Seurat)

# load RDS
srat_rds <- "/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230508/vladoiu_mb_merge.rds"
srat <- readRDS(srat_rds)

# add subtype to metadata
srat$subtype <- stringr::str_replace(
  string = srat$orig.ident,
  pattern = "^[:alnum:]*_",
  replacement = ""
)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-umap-subtype
#| fig-cap: UMAP of cells coloured by MB subtype.
#| echo: true
#| output: true

DimPlot(srat, group.by = "subtype")
```

For sct+merge, the separation between the different tumour subtypes (i.e., SHH vs. Group 3 vs. Group 4) is much less clear (@fig-umap-subtype). All three subtypes are clustering together in the UMAP plot, perhaps with the exception of the BT2017075_G4 sample which can be found in two isolated clusters (@fig-umap-compare&#8288;b, light blue near top left and bottom left).
